name: Chang'e 4 / Yutu-2
first: First soft-landing on the lunar far side
description: The lunar far side has no direct radio line of sight to Earth. To land a spacecraft there, China first launched the Queqiao relay satellite to a halo orbit around Earth-Moon Lagrange point L2 — a vantage point that sees both the far side and Earth simultaneously. With that infrastructure in place, Chang'e 4 became the first spacecraft to soft-land on the far side. The Yutu-2 rover has been operating since January 2019 and has driven 1.9+ km, the longest traverse by any lunar rover.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Lander touches down at Von Kármán Crater 2019-01-03 — first spacecraft on the far side. All comms via Queqiao relay at Earth-Moon L2.
